thm:1.summary: $\limsup_n (p_{n+1}-p_n) / [(\log p_n)(\log_2 p_n)(\log_4 p_n)/(\log_3 p_n)^2] = \infty$. Largest known prime gaps; matches Ford-Green-Konyagin-Tao independently. Erdős's \$10000 problem solved (with FGKT).

lem:2.summary: Bound on the size of $\mathcal{R}'$ (the set of primes that have to be hit by sieving with smooth tuples after the $y$-residue sieve). $|\mathcal{R}'| \ll x/(\log x)^{1+\varepsilon}$.

lem:3.summary: Density of primes $p \equiv a \pmod{m}$ with $(mp-1, P_y) = 1$. Quantitative Westzynthius-Erdős-Rankin sieve estimate over twisted residues.

lem:4.summary: Auxiliary distribution lemma controlling residual sieve errors before invoking the multidimensional Maynard-Tao construction.

prop:5.summary: Covering proposition: there exist residue classes $a_q \pmod{q}$ for primes $q \in \mathcal{I}_m$ such that every $p \in \mathcal{R}_m$ satisfies $p \equiv a_q \pmod{q}$ for some $q$. Reduces the gap problem to a sieve-weight optimization.

lem:6.summary: Computes $\alpha_{m,q}^{-1}$ via $I_k^{(1)}(F) I_k^{(2)}(G)$ — the same simplex moment integrals from Maynard 1311.4600 prop:4.1, here repurposed to weight the covering construction.

lem:7.summary: Lower bound on $\sum \mu_{m,q}(p_0)$ via $J_k^{(1)}(F) J_k^{(2)}(G)$. Same $J$-integrals as 1311.4600 prop:4.1, again pivoted to large-gap construction.

lem:8.summary: Existence of smooth $F, G$ with $k J^{(1)}(F) J^{(2)}(G) / (I^{(1)}(F) I^{(2)}(G)) \gg \log k$. This is the same kind of $M_k > \log k - O(\log\log k)$ bound that drives Maynard's small-gaps thm:1.1; the argument mirrors 1311.4600 prop:4.3 part 3.

edges.note: Existence of a covering reduces 'large gap of size $G$' to 'find $n$ such that $p_{n+1} > n + G$'.
$\log k$ ratio drives the size of the gap that can be sieved out — same role as $M_k$ in small-gaps.
Weights $\alpha_{m,q}$ from lem:6 supply the probabilistic measure used in the covering.
Marginal estimates from lem:7 ensure the chosen residue classes hit enough primes.
$|\mathcal{R}'|$ bound limits the residual primes the covering must absorb.
Density of $m$-twisted primes feeds the covering count.
Distribution control on the residual set.
Singular series $\mathfrak{S}_{m,q}$ comes from the density estimate.
Optimization over smooth $F, G$ uses the explicit form of the $I$-integrals.

main_result.description: thm:1: largest prime-gap lower bound. Solves Erdős's \$10000 problem (jointly with FGKT).

westzynthius_layer.description: Classical Erdős-Rankin sieve setup. lem:2/3/4 bound the residual primes after killing those with small prime factors.

covering_reduction.description: prop:5: reduce 'find a long prime-free interval' to 'cover $\mathcal{R}_m$ by primes $q$ with appropriate residue classes'.

multidimensional_sieve_layer.description: lem:6/7/8: import Maynard's own multidimensional Selberg sieve (from 1311.4600) and run it backwards — pick weights so that primes concentrate on the chosen residue, instead of avoiding them.

Maynard:smallgaps.title: Small gaps between primes
Maynard:smallgaps.role: method_source — same multidimensional sieve, used backwards

FGKT:2014.title: Long gaps between primes
FGKT:2014.role: parallel — independent simultaneous proof of the same result

ErdosRankin:1938.title: On the difference of consecutive primes (Rankin)

Westzynthius:1931.title: Über die Verteilung der Zahlen, die zu den n ersten Primzahlen teilerfremd sind
